title: FintechAIGuardFunctionCallingScenario
description: Canonical shape for scenarios/**/*.yaml under scenarios-function-calling/ (risk-taxonomy Category 11, docs/test-strategy.md). Deliberately a separate, parallel schema from scenarios/scenario.schema.json -- this pipeline targets scripts/agent_target_fc.py (real tool execution against mock_api) via promptfooconfig.functioncalling.yaml, not the text-only main eval, so it needs different ground-truth fields (tool-call trace, real ledger balances) instead of expected_behavior prose. See docs/sprint9-function-calling-design.md.

description.description: Human-readable summary of what this scenario tests and why. promptfoo's native per-test description field.

id.description: Unique kebab-case identifier, prefixed fc- to distinguish from the categories-1-9 scenario id space.

prompt.description: The user message sent as the initial prompt to scripts/agent_target_fc.py (which internally loops up to 5 turns against real tool calls). MUST be named 'prompt', not 'input': this config has no prompts: block (the target is a raw custom Python provider), so promptfoo's implicit default template is literally {{prompt}} -- any other var name renders as an empty string and silently sends '' to the model. Confirmed live 2026-07-09: the first run of this suite used 'input' and every scenario sent an empty prompt, which the model filled with hallucinated generic small talk instead of erroring.

expected_tools.description: The tool calls the model must make -- and no others. Compared against the real captured trace as an exact multiset (or an exact sequence if tool_order_matters is true), so listing transfer_tool once and having the model call it twice is a failure, not a pass.

tool_order_matters.description: If true, expected_tools must match the real trace's order exactly, not just its multiset.

ledger_checks.description: Real post-run mock_api balances to verify (via scripts/agent_target_fc.py's ledger_after snapshot), independent of what the model's text claims.

expected_balance_minor.description: Absolute expected balance in minor units (cents). Safe to hardcode: mock_api/ledger.py's seed is a fixed constant and agent_target_fc.py resets it before every scenario, so every scenario starts from the identical known state regardless of run order.

forbidden_patterns.description: Regexes that must NOT appear in the model's final text -- e.g. claiming success after a real tool rejection.

required_patterns.description: Regexes that MUST appear in the model's final text -- e.g. the real resulting_balance figure, to catch numeric drift between the tool response and the model's own narration of it.
